rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW GEORGE JAMESON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/4/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice was not given directly to the sole beneficiary. Did USC direct the executor to mail notice only to its attorney? [CRC 7.51(a).] (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) If there were twelve remaining statutes (I&A item No. 31) with an appraised value of $2,778.15, and four were sold or disposed of, having a carry value of $1,035.46, how can there still be six remaining and with a carry value $1,553.19? (4) Administrative expenses shown on Schedule E (Disbursements) are not itemized to show what was filed. For example, the filing fee for a petition filed on 6/26/19 was $200.00, not $450.00. Another petition filed on 3/17/20 was also $200.00, not $466.85. The filing fee for each of the accounting petitions filed on 10/12/21 and 4/21/22 was $435.00, not $932.08 or $755.64. (5) The executor paid James Rigelhaupt "for fiduciary accounting" a total of $3,072.75. Accounting is part of a personal representative's ordinary services. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/21/23. Please do NOT file another fully amended petition. (Please see CRC 7.3 for the difference between a "supplement" and an "amendment". The "supplement" filed on 6/12/23 and fully restating the petition, was a fully amended petition.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDDIE PATRICK WILLIAMS JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/29/2023. PROCEDURAL ISSUES on Second Account and Report (filed 09/30/2022): 1. Proposed order must be submitted before hearing. LRSF 14.7.A PROCEDURAL ISSUES on Petition for Approval of Report of Status of Administration (filed 5/12/23): 2. Petition needs to be amended to allege a specific period for which administration need be continued. PC 12201(a). LRSF 14.35.J.(2). 3. Amended petition to specify "Third" report in its caption. CRC 7.102. 4. Amended Petition to and Notice of Hearing must be given all persons entitled with, with the remote appearance instruction. LRSF 14.1.6) 5. Petition to identify who are entitled to notice. 6. Although Petition includes some account information; thus, this petition will be considered as a report, not an accounting. This means that the next accounting petition must cover the accounting period beginning 10/9/2021 when it is filed. 7. Proposed order must be submitted before hearing. LRSF 14.7.A The above procedural issues must be cleared two weeks prior. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT LEE QUINN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/09/2023 (no earlier date available). 1) The original California small estate affidavit signed by John Thompson (Attachment B to the supplement filed on 6/30) with a certified copy of the death certificate must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached. 2) Paragraph 38 of the amended petition: i) The calculation of net cash distributable to each beneficiary appears incorrect, except as to Wanda Lackey's issue . For example, Andrew Filholm's share should be $60,992.21; and Ellen Jensen's issue's shares should be $30,496.10 + $30,496.11. ii) Re Tanya Van Dusen's share: The net cash distributable to Tanya and her assignee must be separately stated. If Tanya only assigned $9,900, there should be some distribution to Tanya. 3) Paragraph 39: i) There should be no distribution of Tanya Van Dusen's share to her assignee, if her assignment is fully satisfied from her share of the cash on hand. ii) James Van Dusen is entitled to distribution once his assignments are fully satisfied. The omnibus clause must state the balance that should be first satisfied in favor of his assignee and must require any excess to be distributed to James without deductions. 4) The proposed order must be revised to reflect the changes above.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHIRLEY CHONG 2016 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To (1) Remove Albert Chong As Successor Trustee Of The Shirley Chong 2016 Trust, (2) Order Any Successor Trustee To Comply With The Terms
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No DE-120 form with a proof of service is on file. A copy of the most recent remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) Re each minor beneficiary identified in paragraph VII of the petition: i) The date of birth must be provided. ii) All the persons having legal custody of and residing with the minor entitled to notice under CRC 7.51(d) must be identified. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHIRLEY CHONG 2016 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition 1. To Determine Validity Of Purported 2021 Trust 2. For Financial Elder Abuse For Payment Of Attorneys Fees For Compensatory And Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) Re each minor beneficiary entitled to notice: i) The date of birth must be provided. ii) All the persons having legal custody of and residing with the minor entitled to notice under CRC 7.51(d) must be identified. 2) If the financial elder abuse claim is asserted against Cindy Chong (who is neither a beneficiary nor the first-named trustee under the 2021 restatement), she must be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 17203(b). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LIONEL WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/4/23. (No earlier availability.) No response to issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 5/16/23: (1) The petition caption fails to disclose that IAEA authority is being requested. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) Bond will be required for a non-resident personal representative. [LRSF 14.16 F.] (4) Item 8 of the petition does not state the relationship of each person listed. (5) Item 8 of the petition must identify the trustee of the devisee trust. (6) If the petitioner is the trustee of the devisee trust, all trust beneficiaries must also be listed, clearly identified as such, and must receive notice. Deadline in 9/25/23 for providing courtesy copies to the assigned examiner of (1) a verified supplement to the petition to address issues with the petition and (2) the proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate with the remote appearance procedures attached. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (If issues are not addressed before 9/25/23, an appearance will likely be required on 10/4/23 for the Court to determine if the petition should be taken off calendar.)

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/19/2023 (no earlier date available). 1) Notice must be given on the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120 at least 30 days before the hearing. 2) No person has been identified as a third party neutral successor trustee (paragraph VI of the prayer for relief). No consent to serve signed by the proposed sucessor trustee is on file. 3) The estimated market value of the trust assets and annual income therefrom must be stated for the purpose of fixing the successor trustee's bond.
